Import torch and necessary modules from transformers (AutoTokenizer, AutoModelForCausalLM, AdamW, get_scheduler)
Set a fixed random seed for reproducibility using torch.manual_seed and transformers.set_seed with a specific integer (e.g., 42)
Initialize a GPT-2 tokenizer (AutoTokenizer) and model (AutoModelForCausalLM) from 'gpt2' pretrained checkpoint
Prepare a toy batch of input data with batch_size=2 and sequence_length=10 tokens, using tokenizer.encode on example sentences that include an end-of-sequence token (e.g., '' token id)

Manually create or extract action_mask tensor with shape (batch_size, sequence_length), where 1 indicates valid tokens and 0 indicates padding or ignored tokens; ensure that the mask includes the eos token position
Define a variable 'start' with a specific integer value to simulate the offset used in the bug (e.g., start=2)
Create dummy tensors for actor_log_prob and log_probs both with shape (batch_size, sequence_length), filled with random values (e.g., torch.randn), representing log probabilities per token
Create a dummy advantages tensor with shape (batch_size, sequence_length - start), filled with random values representing advantage estimates per token starting from 'start'

Implement or mock the actor_loss_fn function that takes slices of actor_log_prob, log_probs, advantages, and action_mask and returns a scalar loss; ensure it applies the mask to filter out tokens in the loss calculation
Calculate actor_loss by passing slices actor_log_prob[:, start:], log_probs[:, start:], advantages, and action_mask[:, start:] as per the original buggy code
Separately calculate actor_loss_alternate by passing action_mask sliced as action_mask[:, start-1:-1] instead, keeping other inputs sliced the same way
Print or log the mask slices used for both cases and the resulting actor losses

Assert that the loss calculated with action_mask[:, start:] excludes the eos token (verify eos token position is masked out), while the loss with action_mask[:, start-1:-1] includes eos token's contribution
Verify that the eos token reward is present in the advantages or log_probs tensors at the correct indices to confirm its exclusion or inclusion in the loss
Repeat the above with varying values of 'start' (e.g., start=1, start=3) to observe changes in mask coverage and loss values
Check that the difference in loss values corresponds to the inclusion or exclusion of the eos token tokenized position
Summarize findings that demonstrate the bug where using action_mask[:, start:] filters out the eos token loss which contains ground reward information
